title: eriogonum brevicaule subsp. canum
intro: Eriogonum brevicaule subsp. canum, also known as the desert buckwheat or Mormon tea, is a plant species native to the southwestern regions of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to help with digestive issues, relieve respiratory problems, and even lower blood pressure. Eriogonum brevicaule subsp. canum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used as a natural remedy in traditional herbal medicine.
